# Risk Rating and Escalation

## Risk Ratings

| Rating | Meaning | Default action |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Green | Matches Trinetix template or an approved fallback. | Legal counsel may approve if deal facts are complete. |
| Yellow | Commercially acceptable only with context, negotiated fallback, or business confirmation. | Legal counsel reviews; business owner may need to confirm. |
| Red | Outside playbook, creates material legal, financial, IP, security, regulatory, or delivery risk. | Escalate to CLCO or designated approver before acceptance. |
| Missing Context | The clause cannot be assessed without deal value, delivery model, data scope, geography, regulated sector, or client requirements. | Ask business owner or deal lead for missing information; do not assume. |

## Escalation Owners

| Escalate to | When |
| --- | --- |
| CLCO | Unlimited liability; liability cap above approved threshold; uncapped indemnity beyond approved exceptions; client ownership of Trinetix background IP or know-how; severe security or HIPAA terms; governing law/forum materially outside approved positions; termination terms that undermine economics. |
| Privacy / DPO or privacy counsel | Processing of personal data; cross-border transfers; DPA; sensitive data; PHI; client-controlled data environments; client requests for audit of privacy/security controls. |
| Security / IT | Client security schedule; audit rights; penetration testing rights; access to Trinetix systems; client requirements affecting infrastructure, monitoring, devices, encryption, logging, incident response, or certification. |
| Finance | Payment terms longer than approved period; withholding tax/gross-up deviations; set-off rights; fixed-price commitments; discounts; non-standard currency; prepayment waiver; rate escalation waiver. |
| Delivery / Operations | Fixed deliverables, hard deadlines, SLAs, dedicated team commitments, exclusive utilization, acceptance criteria, support obligations, special working hours, travel, staffing replacement obligations. |
| Sales / Executive sponsor | Large strategic deal exceptions; client-requested public case study restrictions; unusual exclusivity; broad non-solicit or non-compete; deviation from standard termination for convenience. |
